scene.description: A photorealistic wide daytime exterior of a 1,500 square foot irregular polygon residential common-space entry area in Lafayette Colorado, Boulder County, newly installed as a low-water Front Range xeriscape hardscape. The 1500 square foot polygon footprint is fully covered in tan 3/4 to 1-1/2 inch decomposed granite over concealed black weed fabric. Four warm-ochre sandstone boulders (18 to 30 inches) are placed in a 3 plus 1 asymmetric composition — a main cluster of three center-left and a single accent boulder right. Three tons of buff Lyons sandstone flagstone form low stepping groupings. A short dry-riverbed accent line of rust-ochre 2 to 6 inch rip-rap runs along one long edge. Ten matte-black solar path lights follow a simple straight line parallel to the long axis.
scene.weather: clear blue sky, Front Range atmospheric haze
scene.location: Lafayette, Colorado, Boulder County (40.0004 N, -105.110 W)

materials.under_layer: black woven weed fabric, fully concealed

materials.type: weathered Colorado moss-rock, ochre with lichen, 18–30 in
materials.placement: 3 plus 1 asymmetric composition, center-left main cluster

materials.type: buff Lyons sandstone flagstone, irregular cleft slabs
matte-black solar path lights, 2700K warm LED, 18 in stake
materials.placement: straight line parallel to long axis, OFF (midday)
